heading: 2.3.6 Functions
text: mathematical operations operator. must use known function. example, take square root:function consists name function followed parentheses, list arguments inside parentheses. functions take one argument, take , take none .commonly used function c(), combine function. combines multiple values ’s known vector:functions take vectors arguments. example, length() function returns number elements, length, vector object.Useful mathematical functions include:can even nest functions. example , c() function evaluated first, value used argument length function.Combining several features together, can calculate standard error mean (SEM) confidence interval around mean set numbers:calculated SEM can use estimate upper lower limits 95% confidence interval like :
code:
sqrt(9)
#> [1] 3
k <- c(1, 2, 10, 4.7, 5.0)
k
#> [1]  1.0  2.0 10.0  4.7  5.0
length(k)
#> [1] 5
median(k)  # median value
#> [1] 4.7
mean(k)    # mean value
#> [1] 4.54
sd(k)      # standard deviation
#> [1] 3.501143
length(c(1, 2, 10, 4.7, 5.0)) # nested function
#> [1] 5
n <- length(k) # sample size
n
#> [1] 5
sem <- sd(k) / sqrt(n) # standard error of the mean
sem
#> [1] 1.565759
mean(k) + 1.96 * sem  # upper limit
#> [1] 7.608887
mean(k) - 1.96 * sem  # lower limit
#> [1] 1.471113
c(mean(k) + 1.96 * sem, mean(k) - 1.96 * sem)
#> [1] 7.608887 1.471113

heading: 3.3.1 Load packages
text: ggplot2 one core packages tidyverse. Load tidyverse package running code:line code loads tidyverse package eight core packages within tidyverse, one ggplot2. vast majority data science needs can met eight packages. possible load package individually, example library(ggplot2), ’s usually better load tidyverse package often want use one packages tidyverse.run code, see messagein console like :first part tells packages loaded. notice ggplot2 among list. second part message tells tidyverse functions conflict (override) functions. Usually functions base R, also functions packages already loaded. ever want use package overridden another package, refer explicitly package function name.
code:
library(tidyverse)#> ── Attaching packages ─────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse 1.3.0 ──
#> ✔ ggplot2 3.3.3     ✔ purrr   0.3.4
#> ✔ tibble  3.0.5     ✔ dplyr   1.0.3
#> ✔ tidyr   1.1.2     ✔ stringr 1.4.0
#> ✔ readr   1.4.0     ✔ forcats 0.5.0
#> ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
#> ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
#> ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
